title: vicia unijuga var. ohwiana
intro: Vicia unijuga var. ohwiana, also known as the dwarf vetch or purple vetch, is a perennial plant in the legume family that is native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Vicia unijuga var. ohwiana include its ability to soothe and heal skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound closure. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various health conditions.
